github_integration.overview: Guidelines for using GitHub MCP to interact with pull requests in the bob-shell automated workflow. The workflow prompt provides the PR URL and branch name explicitly.

github_mcp_tools.title: GitHub MCP Tools for Bob Shell

parameters.parameter: Repository owner from parsed PR URL
Repository name from parsed PR URL
PR number from parsed PR URL

tool.name: pull_request_review_write
tool.description: Add a comment to the pull request

parameters.parameter: Repository owner from parsed PR URL
Repository name from parsed PR URL
PR number from parsed PR URL

use_mcp_tool.arguments:
{ "method": "create", "owner": "highorbit25", "repo": "concert-bob-remediate", "pullNumber": 38, "event": "COMMENT", "body": "## Unit Test Fixes

I've fixed the failing unit tests caused by the Flask upgrade.

### Changes Made
- Updated import paths
- Fixed API calls

### Testing
- All tests now pass ✅" }

pr_url_parsing.title: Parsing PR URL from Workflow Prompt

workflow_prompt_structure.description: The workflow prompt contains the PR URL in step 1: "1. Analyze the PR at https://github.com/{org}/{repo}/pull/{number}"

step.action: Extract the full PR URL from step 1
step.example: Input: "1. Analyze the PR at https://github.com/highorbit25/concert-bob-remediate/pull/38" Extract: https://github.com/highorbit25/concert-bob-remediate/pull/38

step.action: Store parsed components for GitHub MCP calls
step.usage: Use these components in all subsequent GitHub MCP tool calls

section.name: security_context
section.item: CVE ID (from title, description, or linked issues)

step.categorize: - Dependency files (requirements.txt, package.json, etc.) - Application code - Test files - Configuration files - Documentation

step.extract: - Which packages changed - Old versions - New versions - Direct vs transitive dependencies

step.understand: - What the DevOps engineer already fixed - What patterns they used - What might still need fixing

step.analyze: - Test setup changes - Mock updates - Assertion changes - New test requirements

branch_extraction.description: The workflow prompt provides the branch name in step 6: "6. Commit your fixes to the EXISTING PR branch: {branch-name}"

parsing.pattern: Commit your fixes to the EXISTING PR branch: (.+)
parsing.example: Input: "6. Commit your fixes to the EXISTING PR branch: fix/cve-2023-12345-flask" Extract: fix/cve-2023-12345-flask

branch_extraction.critical: This is the ONLY branch name you should use for git operations. Never create a new branch or use a different branch name.

step.result: This will: - Update the PR with your commit - Trigger the CI/CD workflow - Re-run unit tests - Assign reviewers based on test results

pr_comment.when: After pushing fixes to the PR branch
pr_comment.purpose: Document what was fixed for PR reviewers

practice.name: always_parse_from_prompt
practice.principle: Always extract PR URL and branch from the workflow prompt
practice.rationale: The workflow provides these explicitly - don't try to discover or infer them

practice.name: use_exact_branch_name
practice.principle: Use the exact branch name from step 6 of the workflow prompt
practice.rationale: This ensures the PR is updated correctly and triggers the CI/CD workflow

practice.name: document_thoroughly
practice.principle: Provide detailed documentation in the PR comment
practice.rationale: Reviewers need to understand what was fixed, why, and that tests pass
